coverage_note: The AI Law Tracker inventory lists ~46 Indiana data-center projects; other trackers (e.g., Cleanview) count 90+ planned facilities totaling ~14 GW. This file curates those with locatable sites and at least one verifiable attribute, including RUMORED/CHATTER proposals that are reported but unconfirmed (no named operator, or rejected at local rezoning) — each flagged with its stage in the notes. Flagship figures are cross-checked against IURC filings, utility filings, and news reporting cited per record.

primary_sources.label: AI Law Tracker — Indiana Data Center Map
Citizens Action Coalition — AI Data Centers

facilities.name: Amazon (AWS) New Carlisle Campus
facilities.tax_note: Part of ~$8B in known Amazon subsidies statewide (Good Jobs First).

sources.label: AWS New Carlisle / Project Rainier analysis

facilities.notes: 2,250 MW filed capacity (reported up to 2,800 MW), ~90% load factor; flagship of Project Rainier serving Anthropic. 909 backup diesel generators requested (6.1M gal on-site diesel).

facilities.name: Meta LEAP Campus (Lebanon)
facilities.tax_note: LEAP Innovation District incentives; sales-tax exemption eligible under HEA 1405.
facilities.notes: Projected 8 million gallons/day water use by 2031 (City of Indianapolis sources). ~$896M in associated LEAP transmission upgrades allocated across AES ($9M), Duke ($210M), WVPA ($627M).

facilities.name: Google Monrovia Data Center
facilities.tax_note: Google pays 100% of its power costs and 100% of new infrastructure per AES filing.

sources.label: IBJ: AES plan to power Google DC
Inside INdiana Business: regulators OK energy project
CAC: IURC must prioritize public interest

facilities.notes: Phase 1 = 390 MW, full buildout up to 1,200 MW. AES filed Cause 46394 on 4/22/2026; associated energy project approved by IURC July 2026; full order expected Sept 2026. AES claims $770M customer savings over 15 years.

facilities.name: Google Fort Wayne Campus
facilities.notes: I&M interconnection for Google (Fort Wayne) and Amazon (New Carlisle) drove >$400M in upgrades (Cause 46301). ~2 acres wetland destroyed at site.

facilities.name: Microsoft Granger Campus
facilities.notes: 939-acre proposed campus; 1,000 MW.

facilities.name: Takanock Goshen (stealth campus)
facilities.notes: Stealth developer Takanock is entitling a large Elkhart County site to sell shovel-ready to an unnamed operator; it intervened in IURC Cause 46183 (Apr 2025). The ~1,500 MW figure is from industry trackers, not a filing, and no hyperscale tenant is public.

facilities.name: Unnamed Data Center (Gibson/Posey)
facilities.notes: ~600 MW proposed in CenterPoint (SW Indiana) territory; developer undisclosed.

facilities.name: Microsoft La Porte Campus

sources.label: Microsoft Data Center Academy w/ Ivy Tech

facilities.notes: 538 MW; Microsoft broke ground July 2026 and opened Indiana's first Microsoft Data Center Academy with Ivy Tech.

facilities.name: Sentinel Hebron (undisclosed operator)

sources.label: NWI Times — Developer proposes Porter County data center
DCD — Four-building campus explored in NW Indiana

facilities.notes: A ~164-acre, roughly 504 MW campus proposed near Hebron and served by NIPSCO has drawn repeated pushback at Porter County meetings. Attributed to Sentinel in local reporting, but no operator or end user is confirmed and the MW figure comes from grid-interconnection trackers rather than a formal filing.

facilities.name: Unnamed Data Center (New Haven)
facilities.notes: ~500 MW proposed near Fort Wayne.

facilities.name: Meta Jeffersonville Campus
facilities.notes: River Ridge Commerce Center; largest power user in Duke's Indiana territory. $216M in network upgrades (Cause 45647 TDSIC-5) shifted to all Indiana customers per CAC.

facilities.name: Sabey Decatur Township Campus

sources.label: WFYI: Decatur Twp Sabey MDC hearing

facilities.notes: $4B, 250 MW on 130 acres near Kentucky Ave & Camby Rd. MDC approved land-use variance; groundbreaking expected 2026.

facilities.name: Digital Crossroads (SLG) Campus
facilities.notes: 260 MW proposed, NW Indiana.

facilities.name: Unnamed Data Center (Hanover)
facilities.notes: ~310 MW proposed, Hoosier Energy cooperative territory.

facilities.name: Unnamed Data Center (Brazil)
facilities.notes: ~400 MW proposed, west-central Indiana.

facilities.name: Metrobloks (Martindale-Brightwood)
facilities.notes: ~75 MW on 14 acres in a historically Black Indianapolis neighborhood; a focus of city moratorium debate.

facilities.name: Jumping Bison Data Center
facilities.notes: 50 MW on 550 acres, Duke territory.

facilities.name: Phoenix Investments Data Center
facilities.notes: 67-acre site under construction; capacity not disclosed.

facilities.name: Amazon Portage Data Center
facilities.notes: $87M Amazon site in NW Indiana; capacity not disclosed.

facilities.name: Surge Development (Knightstown)

sources.label: WFYI — Henry County approves rezoning that could bring data center
Indiana Public Radio — Henry County data center approved at final vote
Brookville American — Developer discusses Knightstown proposal

facilities.notes: Surge Development's 500+ acre site near SR-109/I-70 cleared its final Henry County commissioners vote (Jan 2026), but the developer says no operator or user is selected and a full development plan is still pending. No MW or investment has been reported.

facilities.name: Karis Critical Data Center
facilities.notes: 178-acre proposal, NW Indiana.

facilities.name: Hobart Devco Data Center
facilities.notes: 168-acre proposal, NW Indiana.

facilities.name: Google SE Marion County (Withdrawn)
facilities.notes: 400 MW on 467 acres — withdrawn after community opposition.

facilities.name: Vantage Pittsboro Campus (Withdrawn)
facilities.notes: 600 MW on 618 acres — withdrawn.

facilities.name: Merom Data Center (Withdrawn)
facilities.notes: 1,580 MW proposed at the retired Merom coal site — withdrawn.

facilities.name: Logistix Data Center (Withdrawn)
facilities.notes: 300+ MW on 718 acres — withdrawn.

facilities.name: QTS Jeremiah Campus (Withdrawn)
facilities.notes: Two QTS proposals in Union Township (Wheeler), Porter County, both withdrawn in September 2025. County corrected from Benton to Porter — the prior value was a transcription error.

facilities.name: Wylie Capital Data Center (Hobart)
facilities.notes: 400-acre proposal, NW Indiana.

facilities.name: Province Group Data Center
facilities.notes: Proposed in NW Indiana.

facilities.name: Unnamed Data Center (Kingsbury)
facilities.notes: Proposed in NIPSCO territory.

facilities.name: Unnamed Data Center (New Carlisle)
facilities.notes: 1,057-acre proposal near the AWS campus.

facilities.name: Unnamed Data Center (Dale)
facilities.notes: Proposed in SW Indiana. County corrected from Dubois to Spencer: the site's own coordinates fall in Spencer County (verified by point-in-polygon against the Census county boundaries), and Dale is in Spencer County.

facilities.name: Unnamed Data Center (Wolcott)
facilities.notes: Proposed in NW Indiana.

facilities.name: Rumored Data Center (McCordsville)
facilities.notes: Rumored, Indianapolis suburbs. County corrected from Hamilton to Hancock: the site's own coordinates fall in Hancock County (verified by point-in-polygon against the Census county boundaries), and McCordsville is in Hancock County.

facilities.name: Rumored Data Center (Wheatfield)
facilities.notes: Rumored, NW Indiana.

facilities.name: Rumored Data Center (Gary)
facilities.notes: Rumored, NW Indiana.

facilities.name: Abei Energy Data Center
facilities.notes: Rumored; Starke County enacted a data-center moratorium.

facilities.name: Province Group Bristol (Withdrawn)
facilities.notes: 247-acre proposal — withdrawn.

facilities.name: Surge Greenfield MegaSite (Withdrawn)
facilities.notes: 775-acre megasite — withdrawn.

facilities.name: Provident Realty Burns Harbor (Withdrawn)
facilities.notes: 102-acre proposal — withdrawn.

facilities.name: Agincourt Valparaiso (Withdrawn)
facilities.notes: 249-acre proposal — withdrawn.

facilities.name: Digital Resources Ellettsville (Withdrawn)
facilities.notes: 343-acre proposal — withdrawn.

facilities.name: Prologis Shelbyville

sources.label: City of Shelbyville — Council approves data center agreement (Apr 6 2026)
WTHR — Shelbyville plan commission says no to 429-acre project

facilities.notes: The Shelbyville Plan Commission voted no on Jan 7 2026, but the Common Council then approved annexation, rezoning and a roughly $2B economic development agreement 4-2-1 on April 6 2026 — so the earlier 'denied' status was wrong. Reported at 576 acres and up to 13 buildings; no megawatt figure is confirmed in any source, so the 900 MW carried by some trackers is not used here.

facilities.name: PowerHouse Hobart Data Center
facilities.notes: 450 MW proposed in NW Indiana (NIPSCO territory).

facilities.name: Project Shirley (Sentinel · Lowell)

sources.label: NWI Times — Rezoning approved for rural Lake County data center
ABC7 Chicago — Lowell town hall on $5B Sentinel data center
Latitude Media — Anatomy of Indiana data center opposition

facilities.notes: Sentinel's ~160-acre 'Project Shirley' won a 6-0 Lake County rezoning (Jun 2026) after a Lowell town hall, but no anchor tenant is named. Reported at ~500 MW / $5B and merchant-built on spec, so the end user stays unconfirmed.

facilities.name: New Carlisle NE campus (denied)

sources.label: DCD — Potential data center NE of New Carlisle

facilities.notes: A petition to rezone ~1,057 acres northeast of New Carlisle for an undisclosed operator (reported ~$12-13B, NY 'Broad St.' address, landowners under confidentiality) was denied 7-2 by the St. Joseph County Council in spring 2026 after a marathon hearing.

facilities.name: Logix Realty / Clinton County (denied)

sources.label: DCD — Logix Realty denied over concerns and 'red flags'
WTHR — Clinton County commissioners reject data center
Clinton County Today — APC rezoning hearing set

facilities.notes: Logix Realty's bid to rezone ~715 acres near Frankfort — end user rumored only as 'DataOne,' >$10B a proponent projection — was denied 3-0 by Clinton County Commissioners amid reported developer 'red flags.' It can't be refiled on the same land for 12 months.

1,000-acre park on US 41 east of the former Merom plant; site work underway April 2026. The $65B is a projected tenant investment, not committed capex. Capacity is NOT reliably reported: sourced figures are 430 MW for phase one and up to 2.1 GW at full buildout, so no single MW figure is carried here. Distinct from the withdrawn Hallador/Merom proposal.
facilities.name: Heartland Industrial Park (Potentia)

sources.label: Indiana Public Media — $65B industrial park coming to Sullivan County
WBIW — Potentia unveils Sullivan County project

facilities.notes: 430 MW on 140 acres leased inside Heartland Industrial Park. Financed with $5.7B senior secured notes; 15-year Fluidstack master lease credit-guaranteed by Google. First data hall expected July 2027.
facilities.name: Next Frontier Campus (Coatue / Fluidstack)

sources.label: The Real Deal — Coatue launches data center land venture

facilities.notes: 600 MW load request approved in MISO's October 2025 interconnection cycle (queue #51079, transmission owner SIGE). County is NOT confirmed: MISO names only the transmission owner, and SIGE territory spans Vanderburgh, Warrick, Posey, Gibson and Spencer. It is also unconfirmed that the customer is a data center.
facilities.name: Project Raider (undisclosed)

facilities.notes: Proposed at I-69/US 50 at 250-500 MW (500 is the top of a reported range). Washington City Council voted unanimously in May 2026 to cease all dealings with the developer and explore a moratorium.
facilities.name: Outrigger Industrial (Washington)

sources.label: Indiana Public Media — New data center proposed near Washington
Indiana Public Media — Council cancels data center development

facilities.notes: 500 MW hyperscale proposal on ~300 acres; $100-150M in infrastructure. No site finalized and the end buyer is an undisclosed hyperscaler. Fulton County adopted a moratorium in March 2026.
facilities.name: Decennial Group (Akron)

sources.label: ABC57 — Developer unveils Akron data center plan
ABC57 — Latest details on Fulton County development

facilities.notes: Approved 6-1 by the Metropolitan Development Commission on July 15 2026 as a use variance, over neighborhood opposition. Scaled back before the vote: ~80 MW to 52 MW, three buildings to two, 56 diesel generators to 31. 320,000 sq ft on the east side near Irvington.
facilities.name: DC Blox Indianapolis

sources.label: WFYI — East side data center wins approval despite opposition
Mirror Indy — DC Blox campus, Irvington / Warren Township

facilities.notes: 24 MW edge colocation campus (Indy I and Indy II) with a new substation.
facilities.name: RadiusDC Indy Campus (Plainfield)

sources.label: RadiusDC — edge colocation campus in Indianapolis

facilities.notes: Operating colocation facility: 2.5 MW utility power, 1.5 MW critical IT load, 8,500 sq ft data hall. Small by current standards and included for completeness.
facilities.name: DartPoints CLU01 (Columbus)

facilities.notes: 554-acre proposal denied unanimously by the Kosciusko County Commissioners on April 22 2025, after the Area Plan Commission also denied it unanimously on April 2.
facilities.name: Prologis Leesburg (denied)

sources.label: WNDU — Commissioners say no to proposed Leesburg data center

facilities.notes: 87-acre proposal, distinct from Wylie Capital's separate ~400-acre Hobart site.
facilities.name: Wylie Capital (Merrillville)

sources.label: Citizens Action Coalition — Indiana data center tracker

facilities.notes: 110-acre data center announced at the Thomas Rose Industrial Park in January 2026; La Porte later granted a height variance. Headline and date confirmed via news index; the publishers block automated article retrieval, so figures beyond acreage are unverified.
facilities.name: Thomas Rose Industrial Park (La Porte)

sources.label: WSBT — 110-acre data center planned at LaPorte industrial park (Jan 15 2026)
Citizens Action Coalition — Indiana data center tracker

facilities.notes: 746 acres annexed and rezoned by the Kokomo Common Council over resident opposition, but council members stated on the record that no specific use has been proposed or formally discussed and called a data center 'completely speculative'. Listed as rumored rather than proposed for that reason.
facilities.name: Unnamed annexation site (Kokomo)

sources.label: WRTV — Kokomo council approves annexation and rezoning amid opposition

facilities.notes: 10-shell campus on ~300 acres northeast of the US 31/20 bypass. The land is already zoned industrial, so no rezoning is required — which means there may be no public vote.
facilities.name: Western Hospitality Partners (South Bend)

sources.label: WVPE — New Carlisle developer planning South Bend data center

facilities.notes: Second Microsoft campus in La Porte, distinct from the first. City Council approved annexation and rezoning in May 2026; groundbreaking reported June 17-18 2026 at roughly $1B.
facilities.name: Microsoft La Porte campus #2

sources.label: Inside INdiana Business — Microsoft breaks ground on La Porte data center
